description: Legal document. Partnership settlement. Dated: October 1143. Location: Fustat. Written in the hand of Nathan b. Shelomo ha-Kohen. The recto records the settlement of a partnership and the verso records subsequent payments (signed by Ḥiyyā b. Yiṣḥaq and Natan b. Shelomo ha-Kohen). The partners, Khalaf b. Abū l-Ḥasan al-Damsīsī and Yosef b. Ḥassān al-Mahdawī, traveled to Upper Egypt to produce purple dye and dye purple textiles. Conflict ensued, perhaps when the partners divided up profits or losses from the sale of the dye (since the division isn't specified in the document). The mutual release was complete, except for a payment of two dinars to be made from Yosef to Khalaf according to a payment schedule described in the document. If Yosef fails to make full payment, he must make a donation to the poor in the same amount as he owes. Khalaf is to be trusted (presumably, without an oath) concerning Yosef's payments. Despite the aforementioned penalty clause, Yosef apparently took longer than expected to repay the two dinars, since the final release which appears on the verso is dated six months after the end of the loan repayment period, in 1144. (Information from Lieberman, "A Partnership Culture," 219)
tags: debt, autograph, business dispute, dye, Upper Egypt, Partnership
description_he: מסמך משפטי. הסדר שותפות, אוקטובר 1143, פוסטאט. שני שותפים נסעו למצרים העליונה לייצור צבע ארגמן; הסדרו סכסוך ב-2 דינרים.
